name: Treasury Prime Banking-as-a-Service API
what_it_does: Treasury Prime banking-as-a-service REST API for fintech companies and software platforms to embed banking products (accounts, payments, cards) through a network of FDIC-insured bank partners. Enables AI agents to manage bank account creation and KYC onboarding for embedded banking product automation, handle ACH origination and receipt for payment product automation, access debit card issuance and management for card product automation, retrieve real-time transaction and balance monitoring for account management automation, manage sub-accounts and account hierarchy for financial product structure automation, handle push-to-card for instant disbursement automation, access wire transfer for high-value payment automation, retrieve spend controls and card authorization rules for card program automation, manage bank partner selection and redundancy for multi-bank BaaS resilience automation, and integrate Treasury Prime with lending platforms, marketplaces, and software companies for embedded banking product development.
use_cases: Creating bank accounts from embedded banking product agents
Originating ACH from payment product automation agents
Issuing debit cards from card program automation agents
Managing KYC from account onboarding automation agents
not_for: Credit card issuing (Treasury Prime offers debit, not credit cards)
Merchant payment processing and acquiring (use Stripe or Square)
Investment and brokerage accounts (use Alpaca or DriveWealth)
best_when: A fintech company or software platform wanting AI agents to build embedded banking products (bank accounts, ACH, debit cards, wires) through Treasury Prime's bank network without managing direct bank relationships.
avoid_when: BANK PARTNER SELECTION IMPACT ON FEATURE AVAILABILITY: Treasury Prime's bank partner network includes multiple banks with different feature sets and approval processes; automated banking product deployment must account for feature availability by bank partner; some bank partners support card issuance while others do not; automated product feature planning must verify bank partner capability matrix before product launch. FDIC INSURANCE SWEEP PROGRAM CONCENTRATION: Treasury Prime uses FDIC sweep programs across partner banks for deposit insurance coverage above $250K; automated cash management that relies on FDIC sweep for high-balance accounts must monitor sweep program bank partner health; sweep program disruption creates uninsured deposit exposure above single-bank FDIC limit. KYC WATERFALL APPROVAL DEPENDENCY ON BANK PARTNER: Treasury Prime KYC decisions are made by the bank partner, not Treasury Prime; automated account onboarding with instant KYC approval assumption must handle bank partner manual review cases; automated onboarding workflows that expect synchronous KYC approval must implement async KYC decision webhook handling for manual review cases.

auth.notes: Treasury Prime uses API key authentication with scoped permissions. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2017 by Chris Dean and Sergej Kotliar. Backed by Andreessen Horowitz, QED Investors, Deciens Capital ($40M+ raised). Bank network includes Grasshopper Bank, Piermont Bank, First Internet Bank, and others. Products: Bank accounts, ACH, debit cards, wires, push-to-card. FDIC-insured via bank partners. SOC2. FinCEN. Competes with Unit, Synapse (bankrupt 2024), and Synctera for BaaS middleware.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox free; production access requires bank partner approval
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-transaction usage fees; monthly platform fee; account monthly fee; custom volume pricing
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. a16z, QED backed. Per-transaction usage. Bank partner network. Free sandbox. Production requires bank approval.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; transaction and account queries throttled; webhooks for transaction, KYC, and payment events

known_agent_gotchas: BANK PARTNER OUTAGE FAILOVER NOT AUTOMATIC: Treasury Prime provides bank partner redundancy but failover is not automatic; bank partner outage requires manual failover coordination with Treasury Prime; automated banking workflows dependent on single bank partner must implement circuit breaker with Treasury Prime support escalation; multi-bank routing for resilience requires explicit product configuration with Treasury Prime

ACH ORIGINATION CUTOFF WINDOWS BY BANK PARTNER: ACH origination cutoff times vary by Treasury Prime bank partner; automated same-day ACH must target correct bank partner with same-day capability and submit before partner-specific cutoff; automated ACH scheduling without bank partner cutoff awareness creates next-day processing for time-sensitive same-day payments

SYNAPSE BANKRUPT PRECEDENT FOR BaaS RISK: Synapse Financial (BaaS middleware) bankruptcy in 2024 left fintech customers with frozen funds and reconciliation chaos; Treasury Prime has similar middleware position; automated banking products built on BaaS middleware carry platform risk; evaluate bank partner direct access (e.g., Column) vs middleware for enterprise-critical automated banking workflows

CARD AUTHORIZATION WEBHOOK LATENCY FOR SPENDING CONTROLS: Treasury Prime card authorization webhooks for spend control decisions have strict timing requirements (milliseconds); automated spend control logic implemented via webhook must respond within authorization timeout window; automated authorization decisions that exceed timeout create auth decline; implement authorization decision logic with sub-100ms response for card control use cases

KYC CIP REQUIREMENTS PER BANK PARTNER STANDARDS: KYC requirements for account opening vary slightly by Treasury Prime bank partner (each bank sets their own CIP standards); automated onboarding flow may need adjustment when switching bank partners; automated KYC data collection designed for one partner may be insufficient for another partner's CIP requirements
agent_readiness.security_notes: BaaS middleware. SOC2, FinCEN, BSA. API key scoped. US. Bank account, payment, and card financial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: BaaS platform. SOC2, FinCEN, BSA. API key scoped. US. Bank account, ACH, and payment data.
